report_name: MITRE Report Highlights Evolving Cybersecurity Risks for AI- and Cloud-Enabled Medical Devices

attack_patterns.name: Poison Training Data
attack_patterns.description: The MITRE report warns that malicious actors could use data poisoning attacks to compromise the training data for AI algorithms in medical devices, potentially leading to incorrect diagnoses or treatment recommendations.

attack_patterns.name: Craft Adversarial Data
attack_patterns.description: The report highlights the risk of adversarial AI attacks, where manipulated inputs such as medical images are crafted to cause misclassification by a device's AI model, posing a direct threat to patient safety.

vulnerabilities.name: AI Data Poisoning Vulnerability in Medical Devices
vulnerabilities.description: A systemic vulnerability in AI-enabled medical devices where the integrity of machine learning models is compromised through malicious poisoning of their training data, potentially leading to incorrect device functionality.

vulnerabilities.name: Adversarial AI Input Vulnerability in Medical Devices
vulnerabilities.description: A systemic vulnerability in medical devices where maliciously crafted inputs, such as manipulated medical images, can cause an AI model to misclassify information, leading to potentially fatal consequences for patients.

vulnerabilities.name: Cloud Backend Vulnerability for Medical Devices
vulnerabilities.description: A systemic risk where compromise of a cloud backend supporting a fleet of medical devices could enable large-scale disruption, unauthorized access, or data theft.

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Interconnectivity in IoMT
vulnerabilities.description: A systemic vulnerability class arising from the increasing interconnection of medical devices (IoMT), hospital networks, and EHRs, which creates complex dependencies and potential pivot points for attackers.

vulnerabilities.name: Post-Quantum Cryptography Implementation Flaws
vulnerabilities.description: A forward-looking risk that premature or incorrect implementation of new Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) algorithms in medical devices could introduce unforeseen cryptographic weaknesses.

identities.name: MITRE Corporation
identities.description: Author of the discussion paper, "Cybersecurity Risk Analysis for Medical Devices in the Era of Evolving Technologies," which warns of new cyber risks for AI and cloud-connected medical devices.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: The broad sector identified as being at risk from emerging threats to AI- and cloud-enabled medical devices, encompassing manufacturers, healthcare providers, and patients.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound Traffic from Medical Devices
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Outbound Traffic from Medical Devices
id: 7e5d1a3b-8c4f-4e9a-9b2d-1f6a0c8e7d6f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects network traffic from medical devices to destinations not on an established allowlist of vendor-controlled servers. This rule is conceptual and requires populating the respective IP ranges for the medical device network segment and the vendor allowlist.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/feed/mitre-flags-rising-cyber-risks-as-medical-devices-adopt-ai-cloud-and-post-quantum-technologies
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1071
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    SourceIp|cidr: '10.100.50.0/24' # Placeholder for medical device network segment (VLAN)
  filter:
    DestinationIp|cidr:
      - '203.0.113.0/24' # Placeholder for known-good vendor IP range
      - '198.51.100.0/24' # Placeholder for another known-good vendor IP range
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network traffic from medical devices communicating with unknown or non-vendor-controlled IP addresses, as suggested in the MITRE report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: Anomalous AI Model Inference API Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous AI Model Inference API Access
id: 1b9c8d2e-3f5a-4b1c-8a7d-6e9f0b2c1d4e
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects unusual patterns of access to AI model inference endpoints, such as queries containing potentially malicious or malformed data, which could indicate an attempt at an adversarial AI attack.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/feed/mitre-flags-rising-cyber-risks-as-medical-devices-adopt-ai-cloud-and-post-quantum-technologies
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t0855
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection_endpoint:
    cs-uri-stem|contains: '/api/inference'
  selection_malformed:
    cs-uri-query|contains:
      - '<script'
      - 'SELECT '
      - 'UNION ALL'
      - 'javascript:'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects potentially malicious or malformed queries to AI model inference APIs, which could be indicative of an adversarial AI attack attempt as described in the report.

Adversarial AI Input Vulnerability in Medical Devices
